date_notes: Budget 2020 tabled Feb 27 2020; this article Feb 28. Related: COVID education redirection March 28 2020 (R-089) is a distinct action covering different funds.
title_en: Budget 2020 holds K-12 provincial funding flat while directing school boards to draw on reserves; post-secondary cut 6%
title_fr: Le budget 2020 maintient le financement provincial K-12 sans changement tout en demandant aux conseils scolaires de puiser dans leurs réserves ; les études postsecondaires sont réduites de 6 %
description_en: Budget 2020 holds provincial K-12 education funding essentially flat ($7.209B, down from $7.218B — a reduction of $9M). School boards are expected to draw $681M from their own reserves and own-source revenue to cover operating expenses. A total of 138 certificated and 106 non-certificated positions are not to be replaced. Post-secondary (Campus Alberta grants) is cut by 6%, from $5.5B to $5.1B, following a 5% cut the prior year. NOTE: A separate $128M COVID-related education redirection occurred March 28 2020 (R-089 — different event, different mechanism).

relations.note: Carry-forward from related_events — confirm relation type

update_log.note: Updated to cover both Feb 27 budget cut and March 28 COVID redirection. Added R-089. Both are $128M actions on K-12 funding.

CORRECTION: Previous description incorrectly stated $128M cut. Budget 2020 held K-12 funding flat; reserves drawdown required. The $128M figure belongs to the March 2020 COVID redirection (R-089). Both refs retained.
